instruction: My audio is too quiet and sometimes muted. Please configure the default output sink so that volume is exactly 100%, mute is off, and then save the active sink name into /home/user/Desktop/audio_status/active_sink.txt.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Change the sound volume (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/sound-volume.html.en)；Ubuntu Desktop Guide - If you hear no sound or low volume (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/sound-nosound.html.en)

parameters.command: pulseaudio --start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; sink=$(pactl get-default-sink 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$sink" ] || sink=$(pactl list short sinks 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | awk '{print $2}'); if [ -n "$sink" ]; then pactl set-sink-volume "$sink" 35% >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; pactl set-sink-mute "$sink" yes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; mkdir -p /home/user/Desktop/audio_status && rm -f /home/user/Desktop/audio_status/active_sink.txt

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5);

result.command: pulseaudio --start >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; sink=$(pactl get-default-sink 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$sink" ] || sink=$(pactl list short sinks 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | awk '{print $2}'); vol=$(pactl get-sink-volume "$sink" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | awk '{print $5}' | sed 's/%//'); mute=$(pactl get-sink-mute "$sink" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}'); saved=$(cat /home/user/Desktop/audio_status/active_sink.txt 2>/dev/null); sink_file_ok='false'; [ -f /home/user/Desktop/audio_status/active_sink.txt ] && [ "$saved" = "$sink" ] && sink_file_ok='true'; echo "volume=$vol"; echo "mute=$mute"; echo "sink_file_ok=$sink_file_ok"
